prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `84551f4c`.
codex_response:
Rule summary: each nonzero column is a solid bottom-anchored bar. Any left-to-right chain that starts at a `1` bar and continues only when the next bar begins exactly `height` columns later is laid onto the bottom row; all other bars stay vertical.

I used the Opus hint and discarded the Sonnet hint in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_84551f4c/notes.md), because the Sonnet version treats the rule as “until the next `1`,” which fails on the larger official examples. The generator explicitly builds those bar chains plus preserved vertical `2` bars, so the samples stay in-family. No new primitive was needed.

Verification passed: `5/5` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples. I also spot-checked the built outputs and they keep the expected sparse `0/1/2` bar layout.
